title: fix: media service — remove delete, add Content-Disposition, filename in hash
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Name tags for your files
gist.blurb: We've streamlined our media service for better reliability! Uploaded files now keep their original names for perfect downloads, and re-uploading a file gives it a fresh 14-day lease on life.
gist.summary: The media service has been upgraded to include filenames in content hashes, enabling proper download names via Content-Disposition and automatic expiration resets for duplicate uploads.
gist.impact: When downloading media, users will now get their original filenames perfectly preserved, and re-uploading an existing file will automatically refresh its 14-day availability.
gist.visual_concept: The pollinations bee acting as a cozy digital archivist, putting name tags on files. The bee is handing a neatly labeled file folder to the Monitor Robot, representing the new Content-Disposition and filename hashing features.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny librarian glasses, placing a glowing name tag on a chunky pixelated file folder. A cute robot with a CRT monitor head happily receives the folder, its screen showing a smiley face. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
